source_text: Vous utilisez un modèle de langage, mais ses réponses sont parfois erronées ou inventées ?
final_output: Are you using a language model whose answers are sometimes wrong or made up?
decision_note: Rhetorical question structure preserved to maintain hook tone. 'inventées' → 'made up' (idiomatic en-US colloquial for AI-fabricated content; 'invented' would be technically correct but stilted). 'vous' → 'you' per en-US second-person standard.

source_text: Ces hallucinations peuvent poser problème lorsqu'il s'agit de fiabilité et de précision.
final_output: These hallucinations can be a problem when it comes to reliability and accuracy.
decision_note: 'Hallucinations' retained — established AI/ML domain term, identical in both languages. 'poser problème' → 'can be a problem' (natural en-US colocation, avoids overly formal 'pose a problem').

source_text: Sans un accès direct à des sources pertinentes, votre LLM risque de fournir des informations non vérifiables, ce qui peut nuire à la confiance et à la qualité de vos résultats.
final_output: Without direct access to relevant sources, your LLM risks providing unverifiable information, which can undermine trust and the quality of your results.
decision_note: LLM preserved as domain acronym (do_not_translate). 'nuire à la confiance' → 'undermine trust' (standard en-US collocation). Sentence structure closely mirrors source for AV dubbing alignment. 'informations non vérifiables' → 'unverifiable information' (mass noun, en-US standard).

source_text: Avec ce cours, vous allez apprendre à mettre en place un système RAG, qui améliore la justesse des réponses en s'appuyant sur des documents pertinents.
final_output: In this course, you'll learn how to implement a RAG system that improves the accuracy of responses by drawing on relevant documents.
decision_note: 'Avec ce cours' → 'In this course' (standard OC course-intro phrasing). 'mettre en place' → 'implement' (precise technical register, preferred over 'set up' in course context). 'justesse' → 'accuracy' (best semantic fit for LLM response quality). 'système RAG' → 'RAG system' (word order inverted per en-US noun-phrase convention; added to temp_glossary_patch).
Standard OC learning-objectives header. 'allez' → 'will' (near-future rendered as simple future; appropriately formal without being stiff for en-US learner context).

source_text: Découvrir l'utilité d'un RAG pour un LLM
final_output: Discover the benefits of RAG for an LLM
decision_note: French infinitive → English imperative per OC en-US bullet-point style rule. 'l'utilité' → 'the benefits' (more learner-centric and motivating than 'utility' or 'usefulness'). RAG and LLM preserved as domain acronyms.

source_text: Créer une base de données vectorielle
decision_note: 'Créer' → 'Build' (hands-on, active imperative preferred in OC en-US for practical skill objectives). 'base de données vectorielle' → 'vector database' (standard Data/ML term; registered in temp_glossary_patch).

source_text: Choisir le LLM adapté à votre besoin
final_output: Choose the right LLM for your needs
decision_note: Imperative form. 'adapté à votre besoin' → 'the right LLM for your needs' (natural en-US phrasing; 'needs' pluralized — en-US standard usage). LLM preserved.

'Élaborer' → 'Design' (precise, professional verb for prompt engineering context; 'Elaborate' would be false friend). 'prompt système' → 'system prompt' — word order inverted per en-US adjective-noun convention (modifier precedes noun). Registered in temp_glossary_patch.

source_text: Et construire une application de discussion optimisée
final_output: And build an optimized chat application
decision_note: 'construire' → 'build' for consistency with seg_07. 'application de discussion' → 'chat application' (standard en-US product term for LLM-powered conversational interfaces; registered in temp_glossary_patch). Conjunction 'And' retained — stylistic list closure convention in OC en-US course intros.

source_text: Ne laissez plus les hallucinations compromettre vos projets !
final_output: Don't let hallucinations compromise your projects!
decision_note: Motivational CTA. 'Ne laissez plus' → 'Don't let' (idiomatic en-US negative imperative; 'No longer let' would be stilted). 'hallucinations' preserved as AI term. Exclamation mark retained for energy and tone.

source_text: Suivez ce cours et donnez à votre LLM des réponses ancrées dans les faits.
final_output: Take this course and give your LLM answers grounded in facts.
decision_note: 'Suivez' → 'Take' (standard en-US enrollment CTA verb; 'Follow' would imply social media). 'ancrées dans les faits' → 'grounded in facts' (idiomatic en-US collocation; 'anchored in facts' would be overly literal and awkward). LLM preserved.
